title: Clytostoma decorum
intro: Clytostoma decorum, also known as the flowering creeper, is a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Clytostoma decorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Clytostoma decorum is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Clytostoma decorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. This family is part of the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. Bignoniaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet-creeper family. The Lamiales order includes numerous flowering plant families and is a significant group in the plant kingdom. The Asterids clade is a larger group that includes many flowering plant families, including the Bignoniaceae.
